objective: Run the current Orin NX heterogeneous-video-compute engineering sequence without exiting early unless all tasks are complete, a stop rule is hit, or user/hardware approval is required.

progressive_onboarding.default_rule: Use progressive disclosure for long project context, internal references, and evidence folders only. The oral-template TXT is exempt and must be full-read from the real filesystem path through the onboarding gate before planning or execution.
progressive_onboarding.token_budget_intent: Reduce repeated startup token cost while preserving enough current-state evidence to avoid stale decisions.

current_loop_state.latest_result: The project positioning was refined on 2026-07-23: EIS is the representative real-time vision workload, while the resume-facing project is Jetson Orin NX heterogeneous video compute and device-side dataflow optimization. Existing evidence covers Regular quality boundaries, VPI PerspectiveWarp module speed/perf-watt, MMAPI/VPI/NVENC accepted device path, wrapper/sync/transform dataflow breakdown, format-matched NvBuffer pair small gains, completed NVTX/Nsight profiling, final evidence package closeout, stream-only reuse lifecycle follow-up, portfolio/reproducibility closeout, C++ Remap operator probe, Remap-MMAPI scratch-stage insertion, local-warp quality bridge negative evidence, native-size Remap pad/crop closure, device-stage lifecycle/dataflow V2, dynamic Remap payload cost boundary, standalone CUDA dynamic warp benchmark, and CUDA/NvBufSurface/EGLImage interop safety verification.
current_loop_state.current_blocker: No active engineering blocker is open. The corrected CUDA interop safety verifier passed identity, marker, and dynamic_marker diagnostic runs with rc=0, readable 640x360 output, and p95 black-border ratio 0. The earlier shift/dynamic_shift diagnostics are rejected because visual review found severe tearing despite rc=0. Future work still needs a separate contract before claiming accepted MMAPI CUDA acceleration.

allowed: EIS is the representative real-time vision workload, not the whole project identity
Regular05 source_to_dest is the current EIS-quality device replay convention
MMAPI/VPI/NVENC device-side dataflow is the active acceleration frontier
Regular performance baseline is accepted only inside the Regular gate boundary
resid_r15_s07 is the accepted Regular gate stabilization-strength recovery candidate
NvBuffer pair quality comparisons must use resid_r15_s07 when judging stabilization strength
Challenge and diagnostic sets explain model boundaries
VPI CUDA PerspectiveWarp and 4K perf/watt are module-level heterogeneous acceleration evidence
Nsight/NVTX evidence shows wrapper/sync/transform/lifecycle cost dominates the accepted C++ device stage
forbidden: presenting the project as mainly a better stabilization algorithm
outdoor-car inverse/post_geometry as Regular05 EIS-quality progress
using inclusion_source_to_dest or safe103_crop98 as a substitute for the current resid_r15_s07 stabilization-strength baseline
full real-time EIS without live same-input evidence
zero-copy full chain without measured proof
VPI optical-flow acceleration without backend support and timing evidence

task_sequence.title: Harness static health check
task_sequence.objective: Verify gate, contract, JSON, and onboarding control-plane health before new experiments.
success_criteria: onboard command works
doctor passes or every failure is classified
active loop contract and Regular05 handoff contract parse as JSON

task_sequence.title: Regular05 fixed replay baseline audit
task_sequence.objective: Make the fixed Regular05 source_to_dest device replay the comparison baseline for all following handoff work.
success_criteria: fixed replay evidence exists
bad inverse is marked diagnostic only
main review copy path is identified

task_sequence.title: Matrix convention audit
task_sequence.objective: Align cpu_stabilize.py matrix export, prepare_device_matrix_csv.py, live_matrix_producer.py, stream provider, and MMAPI patch convention before changing producer logic.
success_criteria: each producer/consumer path declares its matrix convention
Regular05 future producer target is source_to_dest
outdoor-car inverse/post_geometry scope remains dataflow smoke only

task_sequence.title: Regular05 stream provider adaptation
task_sequence.objective: Make the smallest producer or matrix-preparation change needed for Regular05 source_to_dest matrix handoff.
success_criteria: producer emits expected row count
frame index starts from the contract-defined first frame behavior
no broad MMAPI rewrite

task_sequence.title: Regular05 precomputed FIFO handoff run
task_sequence.objective: Run or prepare the Regular05 precomputed matrix FIFO handoff on Jetson and compare against fixed replay.
success_criteria: fallback_count equals 0
frame_index_mismatch_count equals 0
output video is readable
black_border_p95 below 0.01
review copy is in categorized tree

task_sequence.title: Live CPU producer convention alignment
task_sequence.objective: Compare live CPU producer matrices against accepted offline Regular05 matrices and fix convention before adding complexity.
success_criteria: live producer convention is explained
major black-border regression is absent
remaining quality gap is classified before further acceleration work

task_sequence.title: End-to-end latency breakdown
task_sequence.objective: Separate CPU estimate, matrix handoff, VPI warp, encode, and wall time under frozen Regular05 semantics.
success_criteria: timing table names each stage
same-input semantics are frozen
no speed claim is made from module-only timing alone

task_sequence.title: Quality no-regression review
task_sequence.objective: Compare CPU baseline, fixed replay, stream output, and live output with visual assets plus black-border/diff checks.
success_criteria: quality no-regression is supported by review assets
raw pixel diff is not used as the only quality decision
visual-veto labels are explicit when present

task_sequence.title: Regular gate device replay extension
task_sequence.objective: After Regular05 succeeds, extend the device replay/handoff check to all five Regular gate clips.
success_criteria: five Regular outputs are readable
no clip has large black-border regression
clips are classified as showcase or boundary

task_sequence.title: High-signal context and presentation update
task_sequence.objective: Update only concise conclusion, evidence, command, and boundary records after measured progress.
success_criteria: long-term context update follows maintenance rules if edited
README/presentation wording matches measured evidence
no internal original content is copied

task_sequence.title: Git hygiene checkpoint
task_sequence.objective: Prepare commit scope when a coherent stage is reached.
success_criteria: only source/config/docs changes are staged if committing
results/raw/review videos remain untracked
commit only when the user asks or stage is ready

task_sequence.title: CUDA/MMAPI interop safety verifier
task_sequence.objective: After the completed standalone CUDA dynamic warp probe, execute one narrow identity-first NvBufSurface/EGLImage/CUDA interop safety verifier before any MMAPI CUDA performance or quality claim.
success_criteria: route matches the refined Orin NX heterogeneous video compute target
identity safety passes before any translate or dynamic diagnostic
stop immediately on tearing, green output, unreadable output, or cache/sync ambiguity
no zero-copy, full real-time EIS, or quality improvement claim is made

selected_future_route.decision: CUDA/NvBufSurface/EGLImage scratch interop safety has been verified at diagnostic level. Future CUDA integration or acceleration work must open a separate scoped contract.
selected_future_route.reason: The verifier closed the safety question without changing EIS quality semantics. The next decision should be based on a new measured bottleneck or presentation need, not automatic continuation.
next_verifier: new contract freezes source, matrix, crop/postprocess, timing scope, and visual review boundary
accepted MMAPI CUDA acceleration is not claimed from the safety verifier alone
any future custom CUDA kernel starts from identity and non-identity safety again
zero-copy and full-pipeline acceleration remain forbidden claims
defer_until_after_alignment: mesh/grid warp route unless a new dynamic model contract replaces the static local correction and has a verifier
more quality tuning sweeps around lim/R/LP/safe103/inclusion

do_not_exit_until: a stop_reason is hit and cannot be recovered locally
user approval or device-side manual action is required

order: honor the latest user prompt and latest human visual review before stale contract state
if a candidate is rejected by human review, mark it rejected/diagnostic before any new experiment
define the blocker in one first-principles sentence
read docs/knowledge_base/routing.md and matched local/internal source summaries
search public web, official references, or open-source code for the exact blocker
clone or inspect external code only when it plausibly solves the exact blocker
if still blocked, stop the loop and provide a bounded internal-AI prompt in chat
internal_ai_prompt_required_when: technical road fork cannot be decided from local and public evidence
industrial EIS practice is needed to choose the correct route
company internal references are likely to contain the missing implementation detail
